2021-11-01.title: Once a Leading Polluter, the U.K. Is Now Trying to Lead on Climate Change
2021-11-01.abstract: On the eve of a global climate summit, Britain’s evolution from fossil fuels to clean energy is on display. But experts question whether its commitment is sufficient, and whether it can persuade other nations to meet meaningful goals.

2021-11-01.title: New Zealand Held a Bird Contest. A Bat Won.
2021-11-01.abstract: The long-tailed bat, one of the country’s only two native land mammals, flew away with the top prize.

2021-11-01.title: Some Australians are allowed to return home as border rules ease.
2021-11-01.abstract: After 18 months of strict measures to combat the pandemic, the states of New South Wales and Victoria relaxed restrictions on citizens and permanent residents seeking to travel back from overseas.

2021-11-01.title: Climate conference kicks off in Glasgow with floods and low expectations.
2021-11-01.abstract: GLASGOW — The first day of climate talks got off to a rocky start.

2021-11-01.title: Michael Bloomberg announces an effort to shut down coal in 25 countries.
2021-11-01.abstract: Michael R. Bloomberg, the billionaire and former mayor of New York City, intends to announce on Monday an effort to shut down coal in 25 countries.

2021-11-02.title: Long Walk From Freedom: Indebted Gazan Trudges to Jail 17th Time. Or 18th.
2021-11-02.abstract: Like many men in Gaza, Wasfi al-Garosha regularly returns to prison for failing to repay a wedding loan. His trip back, always on foot, reveals much about the stresses of life in the Strip.

2021-11-02.title: Day 2 of the climate summit brings the first concrete commitments.
2021-11-02.abstract: The U.S. is expected to announce plans to regulate methane, a potent greenhouse gas, and there is international agreement to tackle deforestation.

2021-11-02.title: Reduce fossil fuels in the long term but pump more now, Biden says, angering activists.
2021-11-02.abstract: At the global climate summit, President Biden has called for weaning the world from fossil fuels that cause global warming, which he called an “existential threat” to humanity. But in recent days he has also urged the world’s largest oil producers to pump more of those fuels.

2021-11-02.title: Shutting Down Historical Debate, China Makes It a Crime to Mock Heroes
2021-11-02.abstract: Under a new law, China has zealously prosecuted even the perceived slander of Communist figures, broadening Xi Jinping’s campaign to dominate party orthodoxy.

2021-11-02.title: Biden pledged $3 billion to help the world adapt to climate change. That’s not as much as it seems.
2021-11-02.abstract: The amount is half as much as what was recently proposed for a six-mile sea wall to protect Miami from the Atlantic Ocean.

2021-11-03.title: ‘My Name Is Cleo’: Girl, 4, Is Found 18 Days After Vanishing From Campsite
2021-11-03.abstract: Cleo Smith disappeared one night while camping with her family in a remote area of Australia. She was found “alive and well” in a locked house, and a man was detained.

2021-11-03.title: A Chinese Tennis Star Accuses a Former Top Leader of Sexual Assault
2021-11-03.abstract: Peng Shuai’s accusation against Zhang Gaoli takes the country’s budding #MeToo movement to the top echelons of the Communist Party for the first time.

2021-11-03.title: Torn From Parents in the Belgian Congo, Women Seek Reparations
2021-11-03.abstract: Belgium has apologized for the kidnapping and deportation of thousands of mixed-race children under its colonial rule. Survivors say that reparations should be the next step.

2021-11-03.title: South Africa secured $8.5 billion to transition away from coal. It’ll be a test case.
2021-11-03.abstract: Developing nations have long said they need aid from wealthy countries to shift to renewable energy. South Africa may show how that would work in practice.

2021-11-03.title: From BTS to ‘Squid Game’: How South Korea Became a Cultural Juggernaut
2021-11-03.abstract: The country was once largely known for cars and smartphones, but a global audience has become mesmerized by its entertainment, and creators say success didn’t happen overnight.

2021-11-04.title: Iran Seizes an Oil Tanker, but Whose Tanker Is Disputed
2021-11-04.abstract: U.S. and Iranian officials gave conflicting accounts of a ship that was seized last month. Some reports said it was the same tanker the United States had seized last year. The Pentagon denied that.

2021-11-04.title: Back in Chicago, Woman Is Charged in Killing of Mother on Indonesian Vacation
2021-11-04.abstract: Heather L. Mack, 26, had already served more than six years in prison in Indonesia for the 2014 killing of her mother, whose body was found in a suitcase at a Bali resort.

2021-11-04.title: Israel Passes First Budget in More Than 3 Years in Lifeline for Government
2021-11-04.abstract: The vote raised hopes that the country might be headed for a period of more political stability after the chaos of four elections in quick succession.

2021-11-04.title: Center of Italy’s Anti-Vaccine Protests Is Now a Covid Hot Spot
2021-11-04.abstract: After leading the resistance to the government’s new health pass, the northeastern port city of Trieste is home to a worrying spike in cases.

2021-11-04.title: South Korea will hospitalize all Covid patients taking the college entrance exam.
2021-11-04.abstract: The decision comes as the country experiences rising caseloads again. The nine-hour test is seen as critical in determining students’ futures, and many have prepared for it since kindergarten.

2021-11-05.title: The Moose Came Marching In, and Stole the Hearts of Canadians
2021-11-05.abstract: In a prairie city, a young moose on the loose crashed into a children’s classroom, sparking a mix of delight and concern on social media.

2021-11-05.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in November
2021-11-05.abstract: Our picks for November, including ‘tick, tick … BOOM!’, ‘The Great’ Season 2, and ‘Passing’

2021-11-05.title: Laughs No More? Australians Are Reassessing a Beloved Cartoonist.
2021-11-05.abstract: I used to think Michael Leunig’s drawings embodied the soul of our nation. Now, I’m not so sure.

2021-11-05.title: A Nuclear-Powered Shower? Russia Tests a Climate Innovation.
2021-11-05.abstract: A remote Siberian town now has its own miniature nuclear plant as a Russian state company tests a new model for residential heating. Some see it as a tool to minimize climate change.

2021-11-05.title: First Black Woman in Bundestag Wants to Change Image of ‘Germanness’
2021-11-05.abstract: After a deadly racist attack, Awet Tesfaiesus considered leaving Germany. Instead, she ran for Parliament with a “courage to change” slogan and won.

2021-11-06.title: In a Region in Strife, India’s Moral High Ground Erodes
2021-11-06.abstract: The hard-line attitude of Narendra Modi’s ruling party toward Muslims has undermined the nation’s reputation as a voice for tolerance in South Asia.

2021-11-06.title: On Spain’s Camino de Santiago, Even Óscar the Donkey Is a Pilgrim
2021-11-06.abstract: An artist and an innkeeper have enlisted the help of a burro in their effort to rescue the traditions of Spain’s ancient pilgrimage route from mass tourism (and selfies).

2021-11-06.title: Activists hope protests will draw large crowds as leaders haggle in Glasgow.
2021-11-06.abstract: Protest organizers hoped to draw large crowds to the streets of Glasgow on Saturday to demand urgent action against a climate crisis that is already flooding cities, wiping towns off the map, destroying forests, and fueling storms, heat waves and droughts around the world.

2021-11-06.title: Tanker Truck Explodes in Sierra Leone, Killing at Least 98
2021-11-06.abstract: The truck first started leaking fuel and people had rushed to collect it, an official said. Many of them died in the blast, along with others nearby.

2021-11-06.title: Spanish Police Seek Missing Plane Passengers After Emergency Landing
2021-11-06.abstract: More than 20 people fled a Moroccan flight that diverted to Mallorca after a passenger apparently fell ill. A dozen had yet to be found.

2021-11-07.title: Fears of Wider Instability in Iraq After Attack on Prime Minister’s Home
2021-11-07.abstract: Armed drones struck the Iraqi prime minister’s home in what was seen as a warning as Iranian-backed groups dispute the results of parliamentary elections.

2021-11-07.title: French Clerical Abuse Report Puts Spotlight on Confession
2021-11-07.abstract: In rare instances, it noted, the secrecy around the sacrament had been used to cover up abuse cases, again raising issues of whether church or state law should prevail.

2021-11-07.title: On Tiny Guam, It’s One Dogcatcher vs. 30,000 Strays
2021-11-07.abstract: They bite, they threaten, they carry the risk of rabies. Islanders are sharply divided over what to do about the dogs. And Nicholas Ibanez is caught in the middle.

2021-11-07.title: To Steer China’s Future, Xi Is Rewriting Its Past
2021-11-07.abstract: A new official summation of Communist Party history is likely to exalt Xi Jinping as a peer of Mao and Deng, fortifying his claim to a new phase in power.

2021-11-07.title: Nicaragua Descends Into Autocratic Rule as Ortega Crushes Dissent
2021-11-07.abstract: President Daniel Ortega quashed the opposition and struck fear in voters, all but guaranteeing his victory in a presidential contest on Sunday.

2021-11-08.title: Police Officer Stabbed on French Riviera, Authorities Say
2021-11-08.abstract: Gérald Darmanin, France’s interior minister, said an assailant had stabbed an officer in Cannes. The motive for the attack was not immediately clear.

2021-11-08.title: When the Taliban Are in Your Bedroom
2021-11-08.abstract: As armed Taliban inspected the New York Times bureau in Kabul, they were escorted by a journalist who used to be a U.S. Marine. The photo of him in uniform was plain for all to see, and ponder.

2021-11-08.title: In Romania, Hard-Hit by Covid, Doctors Fight Vaccine Refusal
2021-11-08.abstract: An anti-vaccine clarion call by leading religious figures, echoed by prominent politicians and social media, helps explain why Romania now has the world’s highest Covid death rate.

2021-11-08.title: New Zealand’s biggest city will ease lockdown restrictions.
2021-11-08.abstract: The lifting of rules comes as the country records some of its highest daily case numbers since the pandemic began.

2021-11-08.title: U.S. Travel Ban Ends, Easing Personal Frustrations and Diplomatic Tension
2021-11-08.abstract: Europeans were kept apart from family and friends as the ban dragged on long after E.U. leaders had opened their borders.

2021-11-09.title: India Eased Its Covid-19 Disaster. Fears of Complacency Remain.
2021-11-09.abstract: The success represents a major victory for Narendra Modi’s government, but a recent slowdown in new vaccinations has sparked worries.

2021-11-09.title: Singapore will stop covering Covid costs for those who decline to be vaccinated.
2021-11-09.abstract: The change, which takes effect on Dec. 8, comes as the number of severe cases has stabilized but remains high, the Health Ministry said.

2021-11-09.title: Protest outside New Zealand’s Parliament assails sweeping new Covid mandates.
2021-11-09.abstract: About 3,000 people marched through Wellington, New Zealand’s capital, on Tuesday to protest vaccination requirements and coronavirus restrictions, forcing a lockdown of Parliament and closure of local streets.

2021-11-09.title: The U.S. urges Beijing to release a Chinese citizen journalist who highlighted Covid.
2021-11-09.abstract: Zhang Zhan, who was imprisoned after exposing the failures of the government’s initial response to the coronavirus outbreak in Wuhan, is near death, friends and family say.

2021-11-09.title: In Hong Kong, a mandatory smartphone app draws pushback and mockery.
2021-11-09.abstract: Hong Kong’s latest “zero Covid” policy — a mandatory smartphone tracking app — is prompting online mockery and pushback.

2021-11-10.title: Malala Yousafzai Announces Her Marriage
2021-11-10.abstract: Ms. Yousafzai, a Pakistani activist and the youngest-ever recipient of a Nobel Peace Prize, married her partner at a small ceremony in Birmingham, England, she said on Twitter.

2021-11-10.title: U.S. Holocaust Museum Says China ‘May Be Committing Genocide’ Against Uyghurs
2021-11-10.abstract: Its report found that the government’s attacks against the group of Muslims in Xinjiang had escalated and most likely included “forced sterilization, sexual violence, enslavement, torture, and forcible transfer.”

2021-11-10.title: In France, the People the Climate Summit Forgot
2021-11-10.abstract: In one area of France, worries about rising energy prices trump anxiety about rising temperatures, raising fears of a resurgence of the Yellow Vest social uprising.

2021-11-10.title: As Distrust of China Grows, Europe May Inch Closer to Taiwan
2021-11-10.abstract: Talks between European lawmakers and Taiwanese officials point to Europe’s increasing willingness to strengthen relations, despite Beijing’s threats.

2021-11-10.title: A Chinese city tells overseas visitors to quarantine for 28 days, then do another 28.
2021-11-10.abstract: The restrictions in Shenyang are a stark example of how seriously officials are taking the country’s “zero Covid” approach to the pandemic.

2021-11-11.title: Unlike Before, Poland Gets Support From Europe on Tough Borders
2021-11-11.abstract: Faced with the weaponization of migrants from Belarus, the European Union is defending Poland’s hard line even as it withholds funds over rule of law violations.

2021-11-11.title: Eyeing his future, Xi Jinping rewrites the past.
2021-11-11.abstract: Senior party officials have gathered at a closed-door meeting in Beijing to cement Mr. Xi’s dominance as he moves to claim a likely third term next year as China’s leader.

2021-11-11.title: Nearly a third of Uganda’s students may never return to school.
2021-11-11.abstract: Recalling the orphans of the H.I.V./AIDS crisis, government officials fear students infecting their parents and keep schools closed.

2021-11-11.title: F.W. de Klerk, South Africa President Who Ended Apartheid, Dies at 85
2021-11-11.abstract: A prominent Afrikaner, he defended the separation of the races before helping to dismantle that system, leading to his sharing the Nobel Peace Prize with Nelson Mandela.

2021-11-11.title: Europe had over half of the world’s Covid deaths early this month, the W.H.O. says.
2021-11-11.abstract: Cases and fatalities have increased on the continent, even as they have dropped or remained stable in other parts of the globe.

2021-11-12.title: Migrants in Peril, and Raw Emotions, in a Volatile European Border Standoff
2021-11-12.abstract: A confrontation over migrants between Belarus and Poland is growing more volatile, as nationalist fervor rises in both countries and a humanitarian disaster looms.

2021-11-12.title: Migrants Stuck at Belarus’s Border Amid Its Standoff With the E.U.
2021-11-12.abstract: Western leaders have accused Belarus’s president of manufacturing a migrant crisis along the E.U.’s eastern border. Several people have already died of exposure. Aid workers worry the death toll will increase as winter sets in.

2021-11-12.title: Myanmar Court Sentences American Journalist to 11 Years
2021-11-12.abstract: Danny Fenster was given the toughest possible sentence on three charges, in a signal that the country’s military rulers would not bow to international pressure.

2021-11-12.title: Migration and Experimentation: What Led to Australia’s Coffee Culture
2021-11-12.abstract: When Italian migrants arrived after World War II, they helped develop a coffee scene that many now regard as the best in the world.

2021-11-12.title: Hong Kong Broadcaster’s Swift Turn From Maverick Voice to Official Mouthpiece
2021-11-12.abstract: RTHK has often set the news agenda with its aggressive coverage of the city. But a Beijing clampdown has changed that, with pro-China coverage filling the void.

2021-11-13.title: Oklahoma’s governor puts an opponent of vaccine mandates in charge of the National Guard.
2021-11-13.abstract: The governor, Kevin Stitt, is one of a number of Republican state officials across the country who has been fighting against President Biden’s vaccine mandates.

2021-11-13.title: As Hackers Take Down Newfoundland’s Health Care System, Silence Descends
2021-11-13.abstract: The province has said little about a cyberattack that delayed or canceled medical procedures and leaked personal information.

2021-11-13.title: Dictator’s Son Says Daughter of Duterte Will Back Him in Philippine Election
2021-11-13.abstract: Ferdinand Marcos Jr., who hopes to succeed Rodrigo Duterte as president, said Sara Duterte would effectively be his running mate.

2021-11-13.title: Deep Underground, a Chinese Miner Discovered Poetry in the Toil
2021-11-13.abstract: Chen Nianxi has risen to fame as a “migrant worker poet,” adding the voice of China’s often-invisible laborers to the cultural conversation.

2021-11-13.title: Urbanites Flock to Atlantic Canada as Pandemic Blunts Cities’ Appeal
2021-11-13.abstract: As housing prices soar in big cities, the country’s eastern provinces are experiencing a surge in migration. Newcomers are being welcomed, but some locals are wary.

2021-11-14.title: What is tromethamine? It is used to stabilize Covid vaccines.
2021-11-14.abstract: Also known as tris, the compound helps maintain the pH of vaccines, extending shelf life at warmer temperatures.

2021-11-14.title: In Cuba, Desires for Food and Freedom May Spark a Rare Day of Protest
2021-11-14.abstract: Young dissidents who rely on the internet to spread their ideas are calling for a protest on Monday, a bold move with little precedent in Cuba.

2021-11-14.title: Queen Elizabeth Misses Remembrance Service Because of Sprained Back
2021-11-14.abstract: The British monarch, 95, has put off multiple trips and engagements recently, and the latest cancellation has deepened anxiety about her health. She has now been out of sight since mid-October.

2021-11-14.title: The String of ISIS Attacks That Killed Three Generations of One Afghan Family
2021-11-14.abstract: Over two weeks, the Rajabis suffered loss after loss as suicide attacks on Shiite mosques in two parts of the country ravaged their family.

2021-11-14.title: After Sierra Leone Explosion, a Health System Is Strained
2021-11-14.abstract: Victims are still dying from injuries after a fuel tanker burst into flames last week in Sierra Leone, a country that has no burn units.

2021-11-15.title: A New Source of Fuel in an Aging Japan: Adult Incontinence
2021-11-15.abstract: Waste from adult diapers is growing by tens of thousands of tons a year in Japan. One town may have a solution: recycle it into fuel pellets.

2021-11-15.title: U.S. Journalist Danny Fenster Is Freed From Myanmar Prison
2021-11-15.abstract: The release was a rare positive development in the country, which has been torn by violence since a February coup.

2021-11-15.title: In Latest Twist in Philippine Politics, President to Run for Senate
2021-11-15.abstract: Rodrigo Duterte entered the race days after his daughter announced her bid for vice president. He had previously said he would leave politics at the end of his term.

2021-11-15.title: Taxi Blast Outside U.K. Hospital Is Declared a Terrorist Act
2021-11-15.abstract: Local leaders praised the driver as a hero, saying that he may have prevented a bigger calamity. He escaped the burning vehicle and locked the doors, trapping a passenger carrying explosives inside.

2021-11-15.title: India reopens to vaccinated travelers as more Asian countries loosen travel rules.
2021-11-15.abstract: The Indian government announced on Monday that it would allow vaccinated foreign visitors into the country for the first time in more than 20 months, delivering a boost to a battered tourism industry as coronavirus cases ease and vaccinations pick up across Asia.

2021-11-16.title: U.S. Allies Drive Much of World’s Democratic Decline, Data Shows
2021-11-16.abstract: Washington-aligned countries backslid at nearly double the rate of non-allies, data shows, complicating long-held assumptions about American influence.

2021-11-16.title: U.S.-China Summit Produces Little More Than Polite Words, but They Help
2021-11-16.abstract: In a chilly relationship of mutual mistrust, staving off the prospect of a broader conflict between two superpowers counts as progress.

2021-11-16.title: Cold and Marooned in a Police State as Desperation Takes Hold
2021-11-16.abstract: Almost a dozen migrants have died in recent weeks in the standoff between Belarus, a close ally of Russia, and Poland, a member of NATO and the European Union, each determined not to bend.

2021-11-16.title: China’s History Is Revised, to the Glory of Xi Jinping
2021-11-16.abstract: The Communist Party has anointed him one of its revered leaders, paving the way for an already powerful leader to wield even greater influence.

2021-11-16.title: The Basque region restricts gatherings as cases rise again in parts of Spain.
2021-11-16.abstract: The move contributes to a national debate on whether the country should reintroduce restrictions to prevent a Covid resurgence this winter.

2021-11-17.title: Mass Detentions of Civilians Fan ‘Climate of Fear’ in Ethiopia
2021-11-17.abstract: An ethnically motivated detention campaign largely targeting Ethiopians of Tigrayan descent threatens to further unravel Africa’s second-most populous country a year into a civil war.

2021-11-17.title: Blinken says fighting in Ethiopia ‘needs to stop.’
Peloton and Zoom were two of the hot stocks of the pandemic. Now what?
2021-11-17.abstract: Shares of companies like Peloton, the home fitness equipment maker, and Zoom Video, the online conference software that replaced face-to-face communications for countless schools and businesses, were darlings of the stock market for the better part of last year.

2021-11-17.title: Sudanese forces kill 15 protesting the country’s recent coup, medics report.
2021-11-17.abstract: The violence is shadowing Secretary of State Antony Blinken’s visit to Africa.

2021-11-17.title: The E.U. adopts new sanctions against Belarus.
2021-11-17.abstract: European Union foreign ministers have adopted new sanctions on the government of Aleksandr G. Lukashenko, the Belarus leader, who has already been sanctioned for fraud in claiming a sweeping re-election victory in August and for a subsequent harsh suppression of dissent.

2021-11-18.title: China Can’t Censor Away Growing Anger Over Athlete’s #MeToo Accusation
2021-11-18.abstract: The tennis world is outraged over the latest twist in a star player’s complaint of assault and abrupt disappearance from public life.

2021-11-18.title: Munich’s famed Christmas market is canceled again as another virus wave hammers Germany.
2021-11-18.abstract: Munich’s is the largest German Christmas market to be canceled as the events struggle to survive the country’s fourth virus surge.

2021-11-18.title: New Delhi’s Air Turns Toxic, and the Finger-Pointing Begins
2021-11-18.abstract: Schools and factories close. India’s Supreme Court blasts the government’s do-nothing response. But Delhi residents continue to suffer from the bad air.

2021-11-18.title: Must This Swab Go That Far Up Your Nose to Test for Covid?
2021-11-18.abstract: Some testers gently swab the front of your nostril and send you on your way. Others dig deep, bringing the pain. What’s the gold standard?

2021-11-18.title: Sex Ed, One Instagram Post at a Time
2021-11-18.abstract: Activists are using social media to do what Arab countries have failed to do: teach women about their bodies. They are aiming for nothing less than a cultural awakening.

2021-11-19.title: Cambodia’s reopening is propelled by vaccinations.
2021-11-19.abstract: Tourism-dependent businesses have struggled to make ends meet without visitors.

2021-11-19.title: In Rare Show of Weakness, Modi Bows to India’s Farmers
2021-11-19.abstract: A bungled response to Covid and a struggling economy have hurt his party’s standing, leaving it vulnerable to a well-organized protest movement.

2021-11-19.title: China and U.S. Quietly Released Captive Citizens Before Summit
2021-11-19.abstract: The moves seemed aimed at untangling diplomatic knots as the two countries are clashing over human rights abuses, economic policies and much more.

2021-11-19.title: In Welcoming Prince Charles, Egypt’s Leader Sends a Message
2021-11-19.abstract: The prince and his wife, Camilla, made Egypt their first foreign destination since the pandemic. Vestiges of British colonialism aside, they saw something new.

2021-11-19.title: What Does Mateship Mean to You?
2021-11-19.abstract: It’s a complicated, often-contested word that has evolved along with Australia’s values.

2021-11-20.title: A Cold and Snowy Match Leaves Canada’s Soccer Team in an Unexpected Spot
2021-11-20.abstract: The Canadian men’s team, which has not been in the World Cup since 1986, is now leading the regional qualifying competition.

2021-11-20.title: Thousands rally against coronavirus restrictions in Australia.
2021-11-20.abstract: Demonstrators gathered in cities around the country to protest vaccine mandates and other rules.

2021-11-20.title: How Are Electric Vehicle Batteries Made?
2021-11-20.abstract: The worldwide shift to electric vehicles is critical in the fight against climate change. Here's how the most important part of these cars is made.

2021-11-20.title: How Hunter Biden’s Firm Helped Secure Cobalt for the Chinese
2021-11-20.abstract: The president’s son was part owner of a venture involved in the $3.8 billion purchase by a Chinese conglomerate of one of the world’s largest cobalt deposits. The metal is a key ingredient in batteries for electric vehicles.

2021-11-20.title: Beaver Dams Mean No Love Lost for Canada’s Emblematic Animal
2021-11-20.abstract: Blamed for flooded fields, damaged roads and the occasional death, the beaver, which has played a seminal role in Canadian history, is now viewed by many as a problem, not a point of national pride.

2021-11-21.title: A Canadian senator has died after being hospitalized for Covid.
2021-11-21.abstract: Josée Forest-Niesing, a Canadian senator from Ontario, died shortly after she went home from a hospital where she had been treated for Covid-19, Canadian officials said Saturday. She was 56.

2021-11-21.title: Who, and Where, Is Peng Shuai?
2021-11-21.abstract: A blackout within China on discussion of the tennis star’s #MeToo allegations has not been able to silence a global chorus of concern for her safety.

2021-11-21.title: As Soldiers Abandon Notorious Myanmar Army, a Morale Crisis Looms
2021-11-21.abstract: The number of defectors, while not enough to topple the Tatmadaw, is growing, galvanized by the nationwide anti-coup movement.

2021-11-21.title: Ousted in Coup, Sudan’s Prime Minister Returns via Military Deal
2021-11-21.abstract: Four weeks after he was detained, Prime Minister Abdalla Hamdok struck a deal to end a bloody standoff in the streets. Protesters rejected the move as a “betrayal.”

2021-11-21.title: Mining Congo: A Global Power Play
2021-11-21.abstract: The Democratic Republic of Congo has been a critical source of strategic minerals for the United States since at least World War II.

2021-11-22.title: Limping and Penniless, Iraqis Deported From Belarus Face Bleak Futures
2021-11-22.abstract: Hundreds of desperate Iraqis are being sent home after becoming political pawns in Belarus’s quarrel with its European Union neighbors.

2021-11-22.title: Australia will allow some visa holders to return in December.
2021-11-22.abstract: The country will allow skilled workers and international students to enter next month, the government announced.

2021-11-22.title: How Peng Shuai Went From ‘Chinese Princess’ to Silenced #MeToo Accuser
2021-11-22.abstract: The tennis star won independence while remaining in Beijing’s good graces. But she has been unable to break through China’s resistance to sexual assault allegations.

2021-11-22.title: Some Pacific nations will take years to vaccinate their populations, new research predicts.
2021-11-22.abstract: Papua New Guinea will have vaccinated only a third of its adult population by 2026 if it continues at its current rate, according to an Australian think tank.

2021-11-22.title: Kenya will impose widespread restrictions on the unvaccinated starting next month.
2021-11-22.abstract: The move, which will begin Dec. 21, is part of an effort to push more people to get inoculated and curb the spread of the virus.

2021-11-23.title: 2 Canadian Journalists Arrested at Indigenous Protest Are Freed on Bail
2021-11-23.abstract: Journalist groups denounced the arrest of a photographer and a filmmaker covering an Indigenous pipeline protest in British Columbia.

2021-11-23.title: Chun Doo-hwan, Ex-Military Dictator in South Korea, Dies at 90
2021-11-23.abstract: The country’s most vilified former military dictator, he seized power in a coup and ruled his country with an iron fist for most of the 1980s.

2021-11-23.title: Burning Bus Crashes in Bulgaria; Dozens Dead, Including Children
2021-11-23.abstract: The bus, which had Macedonian plates, caught fire on a highway on the way back from a trip to Istanbul, officials said.

2021-11-23.title: Are airlines ready for the holiday crush?
2021-11-23.abstract: Even in normal times, the days around Thanksgiving are a delicate period for the airlines. But this week is the industry’s biggest test since the pandemic began, as millions more Americans — emboldened by vaccinations and reluctant to spend another holiday alone — are expected to take to the skies than during last year’s holidays.

2021-11-23.title: As virus cases rise in Europe, an economic toll returns.
2021-11-23.abstract: Europe’s already fragile economic recovery is at risk of being undermined by a fourth wave of coronavirus infections now spreading on the continent, as governments impose increasingly stringent health restrictions that could reduce foot traffic in shopping centers, discourage travel and thin crowds in restaurants, bars and ski resorts.

2021-11-24.title: Raids on Independent Groups in El Salvador Raise Fears of Repression
2021-11-24.abstract: President Nayib Bukele bills himself as a young reformer, but a crackdown on voices outside the government’s control is fueling claims of growing authoritarianism.

2021-11-24.title: Lithuania Welcomes Belarusians as It Rebuffs Middle Easterners
2021-11-24.abstract: People fleeing repression in Belarus are processed quickly and given visas. Middle Eastern migrants passing through Belarus to the E.U. face a harsher fate.

2021-11-24.title: New Zealand plans to reopen to international visitors by the end of April.
2021-11-24.abstract: Emerging from one of the world’s longest lockdowns, the country plans to admit most fully vaccinated travelers by the end of April without a mandatory hotel quarantine.

2021-11-24.title: Two actors leave the soap opera ‘General Hospital’ over its vaccine mandate.
2021-11-24.abstract: Steve Burton and Ingo Rademacher had refused to comply with a Nov. 1 mandate that applied to actors on the long-running daytime drama’s set.

2021-11-24.title: A new era: Germany meets its post-Merkel government.
2021-11-24.abstract: Olaf Scholz will be the first center-left chancellor in 16 years. But how much change he can deliver as the leader of a three-way coalition is unclear.

2021-11-25.title: Peng Shuai’s Accusation Pierced the Privileged Citadel of Chinese Politics
2021-11-25.abstract: Zhang Gaoli was best known as a low-key technocrat. Then a Chinese tennis star’s allegations made him a symbol of a system that bristles against scrutiny.

2021-11-25.title: Protests Rock Solomon Islands: Here’s What’s Behind the Unrest
2021-11-25.abstract: Protesters tried to storm the prime minister’s residence, and he blamed the central government’s 2019 decision to switch allegiances to Beijing from Taipei for the violence.

2021-11-25.title: Where Afghanistan’s New Taliban Leaders Went to School
2021-11-25.abstract: Darul Uloom Haqqania in Pakistan argues that the madrasa and its graduates have changed. Some worry they could be the source of new radicalism.

2021-11-25.title: Suspect Is Charged With Murder in Case of Two Vanished Campers
2021-11-25.abstract: In a remote southern Australian area famed for its forbidding landscape and unsolved mysteries, the couple had set out for a weeklong trip and were never seen again.

2021-11-25.title: Explosion Outside School Kills 8 in Somalia’s Capital
2021-11-25.abstract: The latest in a series of deadly attacks comes as the country is gripped by election instability and by a drought that threatens 2.6 million people.

2021-11-26.title: Hong Kong records two cases of the new variant detected in South Africa.
2021-11-26.abstract: The discovery could complicate efforts to reopen the border between the territory and the Chinese mainland.

2021-11-26.title: Here’s what we know about the Omicron variant.
2021-11-26.abstract: Scientists don’t know how effective vaccines will be against the new variant flagged by a team in South Africa, which displays mutations that might resist neutralization.

2021-11-26.title: Coffee or Chai? At 2 Kolkata Cafes, ‘Adda’ Is What’s Really on the Menu
2021-11-26.abstract: Unrestricted talk, or “adda,” is the main draw at two storied cafes that are at once museums to the city’s intellectual history — and places for debate.

2021-11-26.title: ‘Today’s Outfit Is Confidence’: Atypical TikTok Star Enchants Mexico
2021-11-26.abstract: Middle-aged and scarred, a Mexican journalist is breaking the mold of social media influencers with a message of pure positivity that resonates in dark times.

2021-11-26.title: Europe is being tested by waves of cases that threaten its progress against the pandemic.
2021-11-26.abstract: The deteriorating situation is also worrisome for the United States, as trends in new Covid cases tended to follow Europe by weeks.

2021-11-27.title: In the Midst of the Mud and Destruction, British Columbians Come Together
2021-11-27.abstract: A tour of the flood-ravaged parts of the province finds communities supporting each other following disaster.

2021-11-27.title: Giving Thanks in Australia
2021-11-27.abstract: It’s an odd affair outside America, but maybe the holiday will inspire more of us to pause and appreciate the gifts we’ve been given.

2021-11-27.title: 3 Bodies Found Amid Violence in Solomon Islands
2021-11-27.abstract: The police were trying to determine if the charred remains found in the capital’s Chinatown district were linked to the protests, in which demonstrators set fire to buildings.

2021-11-27.title: Iran Forcefully Clamps Down on Protests Against Growing Water Shortages
2021-11-27.abstract: The crackdown came after the protests spilled over to at least one other city and a major protest on Friday loomed. Weather experts say 97 percent of the country is dealing with water scarcity issues.

2021-11-27.title: Afghan Economy Nears Collapse as Pressure Builds to Ease U.S. Sanctions
2021-11-27.abstract: Afghanistan’s economy has crashed since the Taliban seized power, plunging the country into one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises.

2021-11-28.title: Laszlo Z. Bito, Scientist, Novelist and Philanthropist, Dies at 87
2021-11-28.abstract: He fled communist rule in Hungary, discovered a treatment for glaucoma in the U.S., then became an author and a voice against authoritarianism in his homeland.

2021-11-28.title: The W.H.O. skips forward two Greek letters, avoiding a Xi variant.
2021-11-28.abstract: Under the World Health Organization’s system, the Omicron variant might have been called Nu or Xi, but only at the risk of ambiguity or diplomatic awkwardness.

2021-11-28.title: What’s at Stake in the Honduran Presidential Election?
2021-11-28.abstract: Hondurans head to the polls on Sunday, but widespread political violence during the campaign and questionable results from 2017 are looming large.

2021-11-28.title: ‘You’re Not Helpless’: For London Women, Learning to Fight Builds Confidence
2021-11-28.abstract: After a year marked by isolation, loneliness and violence in the city, many self-defense and martial arts gyms say they are seeing more interest from women.

2021-11-28.title: A soccer game in Portugal is abandoned after Covid reduces one team to 9 players.
2021-11-28.abstract: Players from the Belenenses team criticized sporting officials for demanding the match go on, saying soccer had “lost its heart.”

2021-11-29.title: Snowstorm Leaves Dozens Stranded for Days in a Remote U.K. Pub
2021-11-29.abstract: A crowd had gathered on Friday night to listen to Noasis, an Oasis tribute band. On Monday, most of them were finally able to go home.

2021-11-29.title: Both parties claim victory as Honduras awaits election results.
2021-11-29.abstract: Hondurans cast their vote Sunday in a largely peaceful, orderly election, that was nonetheless marred by deep polarization, technological shortcomings and fears of fraud.

2021-11-29.title: A woman is charged with arson in quarantine hotel fire in Australia.
2021-11-29.abstract: A 31-year-old mother and her two children had arrived in Queensland a few days ago.

2021-11-29.title: Climate Change Is Driving Some Albatrosses to ‘Divorce,’ Study Finds
2021-11-29.abstract: Warming oceans are sending the monogamous sea birds farther afield to find food, putting stress on their breeding and prompting some to ditch their partners.

2021-11-29.title: What to Know About Mining in Congo
2021-11-29.abstract: The government of Congo is trying to clean up cobalt mining in the country. Here’s the backstory.

2021-11-30.title: In Latest Assault on Dissent, Egypt Convicts a Human Rights Activist
2021-11-30.abstract: Hossam Bahgat avoided a prison term, receiving a relatively modest fine, in a verdict that appeared designed to intimidate dissidents without risking international opprobrium.

2021-11-30.title: Dozens of Former Afghan Security Forces Dead or Missing Under Taliban, Report Says
2021-11-30.abstract: More than 100 former members of the military and police have been killed or forcibly disappeared by the Taliban since the group came into power, according to an investigation by Human Rights Watch.

2021-11-30.title: ‘Like Fresh Meat’: Detailing Rampant Sex Harassment in Australia’s Parliament
2021-11-30.abstract: A sweeping report lays out a cloistered, alcohol-fueled environment where powerful men violated boundaries unchecked.

2021-11-30.title: Barbados, Formally Casting Off the Queen, Becomes a Republic
2021-11-30.abstract: The island nation swore in its first president, ending nearly 400 years of British rule. Prince Charles and Rihanna were on hand to witness the ceremony.

2021-11-30.title: See Where Covid Is Surging Across Europe
2021-11-30.abstract: The discovery of the Omicron variant adds further pressure to an already buckling continent.
